The Menu Portal

The Menu Portal opens up at the bottom of the screen.

This is how ab-1 shows you the menu to do things like share your work.

You can open the Menu Portal by setting configBot.tags.menuPortal = "dimension"

Then, the Menu Portal will show any bots in the dimension you specify.

NOTE: It's a good idea to include a bot to close the Menu Portal by setting its @onClick tag to:

configBot.tags.menuPortal = ""

Map Portal

The Map Portal shows bots on a 3D map of the world

You can open the Map Portal by setting configBot.tags.mapPortal = "dimension"

The bots' [dimension]X and [dimension]Y tags will be used as their latitude and longitude in the Map Portal.

You can also set mapPortalBot.tags. mapPortalBasemap equal to a value from this list to change the way the map looks.

Mini Portals

There are two "mini" portals you can open. The miniGridPortal and the miniMapPortal

The @onClick tag for each if these might look a little bit more complicated than you would expect.

In reality, you open and close the mini portals JUST like any other portal.

These bots just check if the portal is already open and close it if it is. Then, they make the portals a little bit bigger than they would be (0.4 means 40% of the screen's height).

The System Portal

The System Portal is a very useful way to organize all of your bots, even if they're in different dimensions.

There are actually 3 ways to get there:

  1. Shift-click on ab-1, then click "systemPortal=true"
  2. Drag out from ab-1, then select ab-1. Click "systemPortal=true"
  3. Change the URL to include "&systemPortal=true"

If a bot has anything in their #system tag, that will be its name in the System Portal.

You can also use periods (.) to make folders in the System Portal. For example:

A bot with a #system tag that says "people.fred" will be in a folder called "people" and the bot will be called "fred"
